turns.text: Here's a drone image of my wheat field from last week. Can you confirm if everything looks healthy?
turns.image_description: Aerial drone image of a wheat field under midday sunlight, showing uniformly green stalks with no visible spots or discoloration. High-resolution details of healthy leaves and soil texture are visible.

turns.text: The field appears healthy with uniform green stalks and no signs of discoloration or disease. The soil texture looks consistent across the area.
I took another image 72 hours later. Notice the yellow patches now.
turns.image_description: Same field 72 hours later, with faint yellow patches emerging on 15% of the stalks under similar lighting. Close-up shows early signs of nutrient deficiency.

turns.text:
Based on the sequence of images, what is the final state of the wheat field's health?

A) Advanced nutrient deficiency with 60% yellowing and 10% necrotic patches
B) Uniformly healthy green stalks
C) Early-stage pest infestation with 15% affected area
D) Moderate drought stress with uneven soil moisture

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: The first image established a healthy baseline (Turn 1). The second image introduced 15% yellow patches indicating early nutrient issues (Turn 2). The third image showed progression to 60% yellowing and 10% necrotic patches under reduced moisture, confirming advanced deficiency (Turn 3).
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The progression from initial stress to advanced deterioration requires tracking changes over time to distinguish between transient and permanent damage.
